results.title: Tata Steel Faces Summons from Dutch Authorities Over Alleged Pollution at IJmuiden Plant
results.description: Tata Steel Limited informs stock exchanges about summons received by its Dutch subsidiary TSIJ for alleged pollution. Company claims significant reduction in incidents and calls allegations unjustified.

results.title: SRPSKA'S INVESTMENT POTENTIALS PRESENTED IN BEIJING
results.description: BEIJING, JULY 10 /SRNA/ - A business seminar dedicated to presenting the investment potential of Republika Srpska and BiH, with a particular focus on promoting the Brod Free Zone, was held in Beijing, organised by the Embassy of BiH in China and the China Overseas Investment Development Association. The presentation was attended by BiH Ambassador to China Siniša Berjan, who invited representatives of Chinese companies to take part in a study visit to BiH and Republika Srpska, which will be organised in the coming months, as well as in investment and business forums that will be held on that occasion. In his address to representatives of Chinese companies, Berjan presented the main characteristics of BiH as an investment destination, highlighting its strategic geographic location, favourable business environment, competitive investment conditions, skilled workforce, access to regional and European markets, as well as successful examples of Chinese investments in BiH. Director of the Foreign Investment Promotion Sector at the Investment and Development Bank of Republika Srpska Zoran Talić and Mayor of Brod Milan Zečević took part in the seminar via video link, the Embassy of BiH in China said in a statement. Zečević presented the economic and investment potential of this local community, with particular emphasis on the Brod Free Zone, its infrastructure links and favourable geographic location. He stressed the commitment of the local administration to creating a favourable business environment and its readiness to provide full institutional support to foreign investors in the implementation of specific projects. Talić presented investment projects in Republika Srpska in the fields of energy, construction, tourism and the food industry, which attracted significant interest from representatives of Chinese companies. The seminar was held in cooperation with the Investment and Development Bank of Republika Srpska and the Brod Municipality, and brought together more than 70 representatives of Chinese companies, who followed the event in person and via an online platform. The Embassy said that the seminar represents an important step towards further strengthening economic cooperation between the two countries and contributes to promoting the investment potential of Republika Srpska and BiH among the Chinese business community. /end/sj/vj/sg

results.title: Mike Commodore accuses Edmonton Oilers amid Mike Babcock controversy
results.description: Former NHL defenseman Mike Commodore has stirred the pot around the hockey world after saying the Edmonton Oilers sent him a threatening email as he continued to criticize head coach Mike Babcock. The claim has only added more heat to the ongoing public feud between Commodore and Babcock and sparked a conversation about social media wars involving NHL personalities and teams.

results.title: New basketball coaches and softball coach hired at Abbeville
results.description: The Henry County School Board of Education approved Da’Quon (Dee) Kincey as the boys basketball head coach and Faith Fantroy as the new girls basketball head coach.

results.title: The Bats of Bracken
results.description: The gaping mouth of Bracken Cave, created when limestone collapsed into a sinkhole in this rocky patch of Hill Country millennia ago, reveals only darkness to the human eye. results.title: Police arrest man over alleged ₦3bn cyber fraud

results.title: Namibia secures China deals on infrastructure, mining and energy with state visit
results.description: BEIJING, July 10 - China on Friday said it would step up cooperation with Namibia in the Southern African state's energy, farming, infrastructure, and minerals sectors during talks in Beijing between Chinese leader Xi Jinping and Namibian President Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah.

results.title: Abia Governor Seeks Stanbic Partnership on Infrastructure
